name: Shiny Gmail Lightweight Telegram Rules Manager V2.6 Lean

parameters.query:
SELECT sender_pattern, match_type, rule_type, priority
FROM gmail_sender_rules
WHERE account = $1
  AND enabled = true
ORDER BY match_type ASC, rule_type ASC, priority ASC, sender_pattern ASC
LIMIT 100;

INSERT INTO gmail_sender_rules (
  account,
  sender_pattern,
  match_type,
  rule_type,
  priority,
  enabled,
  notes,
  created_at,
  updated_at
)
VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5::int,true,$6,now(),now())
ON CONFLICT (account, sender_pattern, match_type, rule_type)
DO UPDATE SET
  enabled = true,
  priority = EXCLUDED.priority,
  notes = EXCLUDED.notes,
  updated_at = now()
RETURNING sender_pattern, match_type, rule_type, enabled;

UPDATE gmail_sender_rules
SET enabled = false,
    updated_at = now()
WHERE account = $1
  AND sender_pattern = $2
  AND match_type = $3
  AND rule_type = $4
RETURNING sender_pattern, match_type, rule_type, enabled;

parameters.content:
🔒 **Auth & Security (V2.6)**

• TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID must match incoming chat
• Private chat only (unless TELEGRAM_ALLOW_GROUP_RULES=true)
• All inputs validated before any DB operation
• Parameterized Postgres queries only — no SQL injection risk

V2.6: IF chain replaced with Switch node. Format Simple Reply merges help+fallback.

📋 **Rule Mapping (V2.6)**

whitelist → important
blacklist → unimportant
receipt / force_receipts → receipts
review / force_review → please-review

Match types: exact_sender, domain

V2.6: Added /unreceipt and /unreview.
Dynamic help text shows correct example per command type.
